title: verbascum × omissum
intro: Verbascum × omissum, also known as Mottled Mullein, is a hybrid plant species of the genus Verbascum, characterized by its unique mottled leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of Verbascum × omissum have been used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, wounds, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its high content of mucilages, which can help to protect and heal the mucous membranes, providing relief from coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments. In herbal preparations, Verbascum × omissum is often used as a tea, tincture, or topical salve to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
